name: Valleys of the Rivers Leza and Iregua — ascent to the Puerto de la Rasa - circular
name_original: Valles de los Ríos Leza y Iregua — subida hasta el Puerto de la Rasa - circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Albelda de Iregua traverse the fertile Iregua river valley, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by extensive vineyards, the Iregua river, and views of the Moncalvillo and Sierra de Cantabria mountains. Routes often feature varied elevation profiles, from valley floors to ascents towards higher ground. This landscape provides a mix of challenging climbs and more gentle rides through agricultural areas. Best road cycling routes around Albelda De Iregua The most popular road cycling route is Valleys of the Rivers Leza and Iregua — ascent to the Puerto de la Rasa - circular, a 51.2 miles (82.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through two river valleys. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Logroño – Logroño Stone Bridge loop from Entrena, a moderate 30.3 miles (48.8 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop that includes the historic Logroño Stone Bridge. Local road cyclists also love the Fountain in Navarrete – Vineyards of La Rioja Alta loop from Lardero, a 36.2 miles (58.3 km) trail leading through the renowned vineyards of La Rioja Alta, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Albelda De Iregua is defined by the Iregua river valley, vast vineyards, and distant mountain views of Moncalvillo and Sierra de Cantabria. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate valley rides to more difficult ascents. The routes in Albelda De Iregua are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 6,900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Albelda De Iregua's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Albelda de Iregua?
A: There are over 160 road cycling routes around Albelda de Iregua, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Albelda de Iregua?
A: The terrain around Albelda de Iregua is diverse, primarily featuring the fertile Iregua river valley with extensive vineyards. You'll find routes with varied elevation profiles, from gentle rides along the valley floor to more challenging ascents towards the Moncalvillo and Sierra de Cantabria mountains. This mix provides both scenic, tranquil rides and opportunities for significant climbs.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners around Albelda de Iregua?
A: Yes, there are several routes suitable for beginners. Out of the over 160 routes, 7 are classified as easy, and 89 are moderate. A good moderate option that offers a scenic loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14947729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Logroño – Logroño Stone Bridge loop from Entrena&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 30 miles (48.8 km) long.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in the area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 73 difficult routes around Albelda de Iregua. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989930261/valleys-of-the-rivers-leza-and-iregua-ascent-to-the-puerto-de-la-rasa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valleys of the Rivers Leza and Iregua — ascent to the Puerto de la Rasa - circular&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 51.2 miles (82.4 km) long and features significant elevation gain through two river valleys.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that pass through the famous vineyards of La Rioja?
A: Absolutely. The region is renowned for its vineyards, and many routes offer picturesque rides through them. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31916888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Navarrete – Vineyards of La Rioja Alta loop from Lardero&lt;/a&gt; is a 36.2 miles (58.3 km) trail that leads directly through the renowned vineyards of La Rioja Alta.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes often feature stunning natural scenery and historical landmarks. You can expect views of the Iregua river valley, the Moncalvillo and Sierra de Cantabria mountains, and vast vineyards. Notable viewpoints include Mirador Puerta de Cameros and Mirador de San Martín. You might also pass by historical sites like the Ermita de Santiago or the unique Palomares de Nalda cave dwellings. For specific natural features, you can explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5136528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;BarrancoDelVal&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6615215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoya de la Cueva&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Albelda de Iregua?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Albelda de Iregua are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example of a challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989930261/valleys-of-the-rivers-leza-and-iregua-ascent-to-the-puerto-de-la-rasa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valleys of the Rivers Leza and Iregua — ascent to the Puerto de la Rasa - circular&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40368758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iregua Park – Via Romana del Iregua loop from Albelda de Iregua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the Vía Romana del Iregua and can I cycle on it?
A: The Vía Romana del Iregua is a significant path extending over 77.2 kilometers, following the Iregua valley from Logroño to Puerto Piqueras. It's well-suited for cyclists and walkers, offering an opportunity to enjoy the natural beauty, culture, and history of the area. You can find routes that incorporate sections of it, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40368758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iregua Park – Via Romana del Iregua loop from Albelda de Iregua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Sierra de Cantabria mountains?
A: Yes, the majestic Sierra de Cantabria mountain range is a prominent feature in the landscape around Albelda de Iregua. Many routes, particularly those with higher elevation, offer distant views of these mountains. The Ermita de Santiago, for instance, provides impressive views that include the Sierra de Cantabria.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Albelda de Iregua?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially during harvest season in the vineyards. Summers can be warm, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler temperatures and occasional rain.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or significant water bodies?
A: Yes, some routes venture into areas with reservoirs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40351435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrecilla en Cameros – González Lacasa Reservoir loop from Entrena&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that takes you past the González Lacasa Reservoir, offering scenic views of the water and surrounding landscape.
tips: The vulture viewpoint is located on the Cidacos greenway, a short distance from Arnedillo. From here you can see the rock that is just opposite, on the other side of the road and where you can see all the activity of the colony of vultures that reside here. Around 200 pairs live and fly in this area. You can see the nesting areas between the holes in the rocks.

San Román is the ideal base for any activity in the Leza Valley. It is the largest town in Camero Viejo and the one with the most services. Although there is only one bar/restaurant, the Montereal, it is always open and you can taste the typical Rioja dishes.

Its municipal term includes several towns and its paths are very well preserved. The houses around the church and the church itself are very well preserved. There are hardly any modern structures and a walk through its streets takes you back in time and to the traditional popular architecture of La Rioja.

The port of La Rasa is one of the busiest ports for the Tour of La Rioja, where it is considered a first-class port. The slope that ascends from the Iregua river is somewhat harder than its opposite, although the distance is somewhat similar. You will find successive horseshoe curves that allow you to gain perspective of the valley.
Important town in Los Cameros, with a very good atmosphere, at the foot of the Serradero, next to the waters of the Iregua River.
